Title: A synthetic jasmonate receptor agonist uncouples the growth–defense trade-off in rice
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates hydroxycinnamic acids to the corresponding CoA thioesters, supplying monolignol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established in this paper; used as a key lignin biosynthetic marker and direct OsMYB55 target
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for lignin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell-wall strengthening and anti-herbivore defense against brown planthopper
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in leaf sheaths after In-Ile treatment and reduced in myb55 mutants; induced by MeJA and BPH-related defense context
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Leaf sheath, inducible by In-Ile and MeJA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; EMSA; transcriptional activation assay; analysis in osmyb55 knockout background; lignin quantification
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native Os4CL3 promoter (2 kb) used as inducible reporter in LUC assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In-Ile treatment; MeJA treatment; BPH infestation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher Os4CL3 expression is associated with increased lignin accumulation and enhanced BPH resistance downstream of In-Ile/OsMYB55 signaling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Os4CL3 was among lignin biosynthetic genes induced by In-Ile in rice leaf sheaths. 2. Its induction was confirmed by RT-qPCR after In-Ile and MeJA treatment. 3. Os4CL3 expression was reduced in myb55 mutants. 4. OsMYB55 directly bound an MC1 motif in the Os4CL3 promoter and activated its transcription, while OsJAZ3/4/6/7/12 repressed this activation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A plausible engineering target for boosting defensive lignification, especially together with OsMYB55 activation, but constitutive lignification should be monitored for wall-property tradeoffs
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Not directly tested for Os4CL3 manipulation in this study; excessive lignification could potentially affect biomass quality or flexibility
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct DNA-binding evidence was shown for OsMYB55 to the Os4CL3 promoter, making Os4CL3 the strongest direct metabolic target in the study.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces hydroxycinnamaldehydes to corresponding monolignol alcohols for lignin polymer formation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate monolignol precursors of lignin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mechanical reinforcement and defense against brown planthopper feeding
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in leaf sheath after In-Ile and MeJA treatment; reduced in myb55 mutants
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Leaf sheath, inducible in defense condition
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; analysis in osmyb55 knockout plants; lignin quantification
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In-Ile treatment; MeJA treatment; BPH infestation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes lignin accumulation as part of the OsMYB55-dependent defense program activated by In-Ile
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsCAD2 was induced by In-Ile in transcriptome and RT-qPCR assays. 2. Its induction by In-Ile and MeJA was significantly reduced in myb55 mutants. 3. Reduced OsCAD2 expression correlated with lower constitutive and induced lignin in myb55 mutants.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate pathway component for reinforcing lignin-based herbivore resistance, preferably coordinated with upstream regulatory control rather than standalone constitutive expression
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as one of the representative lignin biosynthetic genes supporting the OsMYB55-lignin defense module.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Methoxylated lignin intermediates such as ferulic/sinapyl branch precursors
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates lignin pathway intermediates to support guaiacyl/syringyl monolignol formation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies methoxylated intermediates for lignin end-product formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defensive lignification associated with BPH resistance
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in leaf sheath after In-Ile and MeJA treatment; reduced in myb55 mutants
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; analysis in osmyb55 knockout plants; lignin measurements
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In-Ile treatment; MeJA treatment; BPH infestation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports In-Ile/OsMYB55-triggered lignin accumulation and associated BPH resistance
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsCOMT was significantly induced by In-Ile in leaf sheaths. 2. RT-qPCR confirmed induction by In-Ile and MeJA. 3. Its transcript abundance was reduced in myb55 mutants under defense-inducing conditions, paralleling reduced lignin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential lignin engineering node for herbivore defense enhancement, though impact on lignin composition and agronomic traits should be considered
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a representative OsMYB55-dependent lignin biosynthetic gene rather than individually functionally manipulated.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anti-herbivore defense against brown planthopper via cell-wall lignification
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential activator required for In-Ile-induced lignin accumulation and BPH resistance
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation of lignin biosynthetic genes; activity is suppressed by JAZ repressors and released by selective JA agonist signaling
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Os4CL3; lignin biosynthetic gene module including OsCAD2 and OsCOMT
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates Os4CL3 directly and positively regulates OsCAD2 and OsCOMT expression; promotes lignin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: In-Ile-triggered jasmonate receptor signaling; BPH infestation; repression by OsJAZ2/3/4/6/7/12
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly upregulated in leaf sheaths after In-Ile treatment; also induced under BPH infestation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Leaf sheath, inducible by In-Ile and BPH
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; RNA-seq; BPH bioassays; lignin quantification and staining; BiFC; Co-IP; EMSA; protoplast transactivation assay
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: OsJAZ2; OsJAZ3; OsJAZ4; OsJAZ6; OsJAZ7; OsJAZ12
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions in JAZ-repressor complexes prior to agonist-triggered JAZ degradation
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native gene in rice; Os4CL3 promoter used as reporter target
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In-Ile treatment; MeJA treatment; BPH infestation; field and laboratory conditions
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes lignin accumulation and enhances resistance to brown planthopper; required for In-Ile-induced defense without detectable growth penalty
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsMYB55 was one of the TFs upregulated by In-Ile and was also induced by BPH. 2. OsMYB55 physically interacted with several OsJAZ proteins, especially In-Ile-binding OsJAZ3/4/6/7/12. 3. osmyb55 mutants were more susceptible to BPH and had lower lignin accumulation. 4. In-Ile failed to enhance lignin accumulation or BPH resistance in osmyb55 mutants. 5. OsMYB55 directly bound the Os4CL3 promoter MC1 motif and activated transcription.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target to enhance lignin-based planthopper resistance with potentially limited growth penalty, especially if activated through selective JA signaling rather than constitutive overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: OsMYB55 knockout did not affect rice growth in this study; overexpression tradeoffs were not tested
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as the core regulatory node of the selective defense module activated by In-Ile.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: JAZ/TIFY family repressor with Jas domain
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Part of the In-Ile-responsive JAZ set that suppresses OsMYB55 and is degraded upon agonist treatment
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcription factor repression relieved by COI-dependent degradation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Suppresses OsMYB55 transactivation of Os4CL3 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: In-Ile/free-acid binding with OsCOI1a/OsCOI2 receptor complexes
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Reporter degradation assayed in roots using Ubi:OsJAZ3-GUS; implicated in leaf sheath defense module
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pull-down coreceptor assay; GUS degradation assay; BiFC; Co-IP; protoplast transcriptional repression assay
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: COI-JAZ coreceptor complex and JAZ-OsMYB55 repression complex
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a negative regulator whose degradation contributes to In-Ile-induced lignin biosynthesis and BPH resistance
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. In-Ile selectively promoted OsCOI1a/2-OsJAZ3 interaction. 2. In-Ile triggered degradation of OsJAZ3-GUS in vivo. 3. OsJAZ3 physically interacted with OsMYB55. 4. OsJAZ3 repressed OsMYB55 activation of the Os4CL3 promoter.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for editing or combinatorial weakening to strengthen JA defense output in rice
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely participates in broader JA signaling, so strong loss could have pleiotropic effects
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of the leaf-sheath-expressed JAZs proposed to mediate BPH defense.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: JAZ/TIFY family repressor with Jas domain
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Part of the selective In-Ile-sensitive JAZ module upstream of OsMYB55
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Suppresses OsMYB55-mediated activation of lignin biosynthetic transcription
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: In-Ile perception by OsCOI1a/2-containing receptor complexes
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Reporter degradation assayed in roots with Ubi:OsJAZ4-GUS; part of leaf-sheath defense signaling module
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pull-down assay; GUS degradation assay; BiFC; Co-IP; transactivation repression assay
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: COI-JAZ receptor complex and JAZ-OsMYB55 repression complex
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negative regulator released by In-Ile signaling to promote lignin accumulation and BPH resistance
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. In-Ile promoted receptor interaction with OsJAZ4. 2. OsJAZ4-GUS was degraded after In-Ile treatment. 3. OsJAZ4 interacted with OsMYB55. 4. OsJAZ4 reduced OsMYB55-driven Os4CL3 promoter activity.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate JAZ for targeted weakening in defense-oriented rice engineering
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible JA pleiotropy if broadly disrupted
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included in the proposed OsCOI1a/2-OsJAZs(3,4,6,7,12)-OsMYB55 module.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: JAZ/TIFY family repressor with Jas domain
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Part of the In-Ile-targeted defense receptor module
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: JAZ repression relieved by agonist-triggered degradation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Suppresses OsMYB55-mediated activation of lignin pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Part of leaf-sheath-expressed JAZ set discussed in defense module
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pull-down assay; BiFC; Co-IP; transactivation repression assay
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negative regulator whose targeting by In-Ile contributes to lignin-mediated defense
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsJAZ6 was one of the OsJAZ proteins bound by In-Ile-responsive receptor complexes. 2. OsJAZ6 interacted with OsMYB55. 3. Cotransformation with OsJAZ6 reduced OsMYB55-dependent Os4CL3-LUC activation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential contributor to stacked JAZ editing for BPH resistance
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not individually mutated in this paper.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: JAZ/TIFY family repressor with Jas domain
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Included in the proposed five-JAZ defense module targeted by In-Ile
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Repressor relieved by selective agonist signaling
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses OsMYB55 activity on lignin biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: In-Ile-responsive OsCOI2 interaction at higher selectivity profile; proposed In-Ile module component
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pull-down assay; BiFC; Co-IP; transactivation repression assay
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negative regulator contributing to selective defense activation when relieved by In-Ile
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsJAZ7 was among the In-Ile-associated JAZ partners in the proposed receptor module. 2. OsJAZ7 interacted with OsMYB55. 3. OsJAZ7 reduced OsMYB55-mediated Os4CL3 promoter activity in protoplasts.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential component of multiplex JAZ editing for stronger BPH resistance
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper suggests testing quintuple jaz mutants including OsJAZ7 in future work.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: JAZ/TIFY family repressor with Jas domain
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Part of the selective In-Ile receptor/repressor module upstream of OsMYB55
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: JAZ repression released after agonist perception
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Suppresses OsMYB55-mediated activation of Os4CL3 and related lignin genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pull-down assay; BiFC; Co-IP; transactivation repression assay
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a negative regulator whose relief contributes to lignin-mediated defense
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsJAZ12 was an In-Ile-binding receptor partner. 2. OsJAZ12 interacted with OsMYB55. 3. OsJAZ12 reduced OsMYB55-driven Os4CL3 promoter activity.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful candidate for combinatorial editing to activate selective defense outputs
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Part of the authors’ proposed leaf sheath-expressed five-JAZ defense module.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Perception of JA agonist signaling leading to derepression of defense transcription
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Defense-associated receptor component selectively activated by In-Ile
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Ligand-dependent receptor/coreceptor formation promoting JAZ degradation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Indirectly activates lignin biosynthetic genes by promoting degradation of JAZ repressors that inhibit OsMYB55
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pull-down coreceptor assays with synthetic agonists
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Perceives In-Ile in defense receptor module, contributing to lignin-mediated BPH resistance without growth penalty
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. In-Ile selectively promoted interactions of OsCOI1a with a subset of OsJAZ proteins. 2. This receptor selectivity correlated with activation of defense rather than growth inhibition.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Selective receptor targeting by synthetic agonists offers an alternative to genetic editing for activating defense without yield cost
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: None observed for selective agonist targeting; broader activation of COI pathways can incur growth penalties
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The accession number was not listed in the provided accession section.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Perception of selective JA agonist signal leading to JAZ degradation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major defense-related receptor component engaged by In-Ile
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Ligand-dependent receptor/coreceptor formation promoting JAZ degradation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Indirect positive effect on lignin biosynthesis through JAZ degradation and OsMYB55 release
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pull-down coreceptor assays with synthetic agonists
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Participates in selective defense signaling that increases lignin and BPH resistance without major growth inhibition
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsCOI2 showed broad interaction with JAZ proteins and was strongly represented in the In-Ile-responsive receptor set. 2. The selective engagement of OsCOI2-associated JAZs helps explain In-Ile-induced defense activation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports receptor-targeted chemical defense induction strategies in rice
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Prior work cited by authors supports its defense specialization; current paper uses it mechanistically in the selective receptor module.
